title: The Helpers House – A Kingdom Governing Community
text:
HOME
MEDIA
CANADA
CONTACT
A KINGDOM GOVERNING COMMUNITY
...helping people and helping society by the power of the Spirit.
Read More
Welcome to The Helper's House
The Church is God’s Chosen Medium for Communicating, Conveying and Conferring the Benefits of Heaven on Mankind.
Vision Statement:
...to make people like Christ and make our atmosphere like heaven.
(Rom 8:29; Eph 4:13; Mt 6:10; Rom 14:17)
Out Mision:
REACH out to people and bring them into God’s family.
TEACH people to know God as Father and how to find, follow and fulfil their purpose in Christ. 
RELEASE believers as God’s agents of deliverance to influence their area of purpose.
OUR CORE VALUES:
These represent the parameters by which we evaluate all we do.
Truth: John 14:6; John 17:17
Relationship: Psalms 133:1, John 13:35
Excellence: Proverbs 17:27; Daniel 6:3
Advancement: Deuteronomy 1:6; Hebrews 13:14
Training: 2Timothy 3:16; John 8:31-32
Our core values are represented by the acronym: TREAT
THE DRIVING PHILOSOPHY FOR THH VISION & MISSION: CARE & BRING
BRING TO GOD (MAKE HAPPEN, LEAD TO, ATTRACT, TRANSPORT)
Skill set: People skill, social skills, and Leadership skills, marketing, evangelism.

RE – CONNECT THEM TO GOD (GET THE BACK IN FELLOWSHIP/FRIENDSHIP  WITH GOD) Skill set: Worship, prayer, bible knowledge, social skills.

INSTRUCT THEM IN THE WORD (TRAIN, TEACH, EDUCATE, COACH, EQUIP) Skill set: teaching, facilitating, training, and mentoring.

NURTURE THEM IN THE WORD (ENCOURAGE, SUPPORT, DEVELOP, CHERISH, CARE FOR) Skill set: Encouragement, fruit of the spirit, friendliness, Word knowledge.

GROUND IN A GROUP IN CHURCH (POSITION, ESTABLISH, KEEP IN,
FOUNDATION) Skill set: discipline, role modelling
REACH
TEACH
RELEASE

SERVING GOD BY HELPING PEOPLE & SOCIETY
We must be bring LIGHT (enlightenment, development and direction) to our communities, and we are SALT (preservation, taste,desirable flavour) to oour communities
Matthew 20:25-28
(The Living Bible)
25 But Jesus called them together and said, “Among the heathen, kings are tyrants and each minor official lords it over those beneath him. 26 But among you it is quite different. Anyone wanting to be a leader among you must be your servant. 27 And if you want to be right at the top, you must serve like a slave. 28 Your attitude[a] must be like my own, for I, the Messiah,* did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give my life as a ransom for many.”
Read More
Galatians 5:22-23
(The Message)
22-23 But what happens when we live God’s way? He brings gifts into our lives, much the same way that fruit appears in an orchard—things like affection for others, exuberance about life, serenity. We develop a willingness to stick with things, a sense of compassion in the heart, and a conviction that a basic holiness permeates things and people. We find ourselves involved in loyal commitments, not needing to force our way in life, able to marshal and direct our energies wisely.
23-24 Legalism is helpless in bringing this about; it only gets in the way. Among those who belong to Christ, everything connected with getting our own way and mindlessly responding to what everyone else calls necessities is killed off for good—crucified
Read More
Luke 12:42
(Weymoth’s)
42 "Who, then," replied the
Lord, "is the faithful and
intelligent steward whom his
Master will put in charge of His
household to serve out their
rations at the proper times?
